role_title: Group Product Manager, Core Infrastructure & Reliability
secondary_archetype: Technical PM focused on cloud infrastructure and platform reliability

seniority.level: Staff / Principal PM (Group PM, likely equivalent to Director-level IC)
level_signals: 5+ years focused on cloud infrastructure at scale
Owns multi-year product vision and strategy
Presents to executive and senior leadership
Comp range $243,865–$286,900 base consistent with senior/staff IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of product management experience, with 5+ years focused on cloud infrastructure, platform engineering, or infrastructure reliability at scale.
with 5+ years focused on cloud infrastructure, platform engineering, or infrastructure reliability at scale.
Track record of defining and executing multi-year infrastructure platform strategies that delivered measurable improvements in reliability, cost efficiency, or engineering velocity.
Technical depth to engage with engineers on compute, networking, storage, Kubernetes, multi-cloud architecture, and distributed systems design.
Demonstrated ability to lead complex, cross-functional initiatives in regulated, high-trust environments.
Proven ability to articulate complex infrastructure strategy, cost tradeoffs, and long-term platform bets to senior and executive leadership.
Utilizes generative AI responsibly, maintaining human oversight to deliver business-ready outputs and drive measurable improvements in workflow efficiency, cost, and quality.

scope.surface: Core Infrastructure & Reliability — compute, networking, multi-region/multi-cloud architecture, platform reliability, developer experience for infrastructure consumption
scope.business_model: B2C and B2B crypto exchange and financial platform
core_responsibilities: Own the product strategy and roadmap for Core Infrastructure, spanning compute, networking, multi-region and multi-cloud architecture, and platform reliability.

Strengthen infrastructure reliability and resilience programs, defining platform-level SLOs, capacity planning, failover capabilities, and incident reduction targets to meet the uptime demands of a global financial platform.

Lead evaluation and adoption of cloud infrastructure technologies (Kubernetes, service mesh, distributed storage, observability, infrastructure-as-code), making build-vs-buy decisions that balance cost, speed, and long-term scalability.
Align infrastructure investments with business priorities, optimize cloud spend, and ensure infrastructure services meet regulatory and compliance requirements across operating jurisdictions.

Shape how Coinbase's product and engineering teams consume infrastructure by building self-serve capabilities, improving developer experience, and reducing friction across provisioning, deployment, and observability workflows.

Drive infrastructure cost strategy by analyzing utilization patterns, identifying the highest-leverage optimization opportunities, and translating infrastructure metrics into executive-level investment cases.

green_flags: Clear product ownership scope with specific technical domains named
Role powers a high-stakes platform supporting hundreds of billions in annual transaction volume — significant impact
Remote-first company with structured quarterly in-person 'surges' rather than mandatory daily office
Equity and bonus eligibility mentioned on top of base
red_flags: No nice-to-haves listed — requirements may be treated as strictly mandatory with little flexibility
10+ years PM experience with 5+ years in infra is a narrow and demanding bar that limits the candidate pool
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in the JD
AI-conducted initial screening interviews may create an impersonal early candidate experience
'Remote-first but not remote-only' language with quarterly surges — exact travel burden and cadence not fully defined

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Group PM', comp range, and years requirements)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; inferred as false — no direct reports mentioned)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (stated as 'Base salary varies by location' implying differential)
ambiguities: JD states 'remote-first, but not remote-only' and references quarterly surges, but does not specify a fixed office-time percentage; location.type set to 'remote-us' as the dominant work mode, but this could be interpreted as hybrid depending on surge frequency.
The JD does not distinguish between US-only remote eligibility vs. global; set to 'remote-us' given USD compensation and US-specific legal disclosures, but global eligibility is ambiguous.

No preferred/nice-to-have section exists; all listed requirements treated as hard must-haves. Generative AI usage requirement could be considered a soft cultural expectation rather than a hard technical requirement — flagged as ambiguous.
